name: Ancient-Blooded Dwarf
description: Dormant ancestral power flows through you; you can call on ancient eldritch resistances that predate the current age.

name: Death Warden Dwarf
description: Your ancestors were guardians against undeath; you gain natural resistance to negative energy and void effects.

name: Elemental Heart Dwarf
description: Your body resonates with elemental power; choose an element and gain resistance to its corresponding energy type.

name: Forge Dwarf
description: Generations of forge work have made you remarkably resistant to heat; you have fire resistance.

name: Rock Dwarf
description: Your stout physique and connection to stone makes it difficult to move or knock you around.

name: Strong-Blooded Dwarf
description: Your robust physique lets you shrug off toxins; you gain poison resistance and a bonus to Fortitude saves against poison.

name: Ancient Elf
description: Your centuries of experience have honed your skills; you gain the benefits of a multiclass dedication archetype of your choice.

name: Arctic Elf
description: You dwell in the frozen north; you gain cold resistance and are not affected by severe environmental cold.

name: Cavern Elf
description: Raised in the Darklands, you can see in absolute darkness as if it were dim light (darkvision).

name: Desert Elf
description: You hail from arid regions; you gain fire resistance and are unaffected by severe environmental heat.

name: Seer Elf
description: You have an innate talent for perceiving magical auras; you can cast detect magic as an occult innate cantrip.

name: Whisper Elf
description: Your hearing is preternaturally sharp; you can use hearing as a precise sense at the same range as your vision.

name: Woodland Elf
description: You live in forests and move through dense undergrowth without penalty; you gain a climb Speed of 10 feet.

name: Chameleon Gnome
description: Your skin and hair shift color to blend with your surroundings; you can change your coloration at will.

name: Fey-Touched Gnome
description: Your connection to the First World is especially strong; you gain an innate primal cantrip of your choice.

name: Sensate Gnome
description: Your senses are preternaturally sharp; you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception and never treat non-magical darkness as greater than dim light.

name: Umbral Gnome
description: Your time in or connection to the Shadow Plane has given you an affinity for shadow magic and darkvision.

name: Wellspring Gnome
description: A wellspring of magic flows through you, granting you an additional innate cantrip from a chosen tradition.

name: Charhide Goblin
description: Your ancestors endured many burns; your hide is unnaturally resistant to fire, granting fire resistance.

name: Irongut Goblin
description: Your stomach is especially hardy; you can eat food that would sicken other creatures without ill effects.

name: Razortooth Goblin
description: Your oversized jaw and sharp teeth are your deadliest weapons; your bite deals 1d6 piercing damage.

name: Snow Goblin
description: You are acclimated to icy environments and are resistant to cold damage and severe cold environments.

name: Tailed Goblin
description: You have a powerful tail that helps with balance; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics to Climb.

name: Treedweller Goblin
description: You have adapted to living in the treetops; you gain a climb Speed of 25 feet.

name: Unbreakable Goblin
description: You're tougher than the average goblin; you gain 10 additional Hit Points and resistance to falling damage.

name: Gutsy Halfling
description: Your upbeat nature makes you resistant to fear; you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against fear.

name: Hillock Halfling
description: You recover easily from wounds; when you rest, you regain additional Hit Points equal to your level.

name: Nomadic Halfling
description: Your people traveled constantly; you speak two additional languages of your choice.

name: Observant Halfling
description: Your keen eyes and quick mind make you exceptionally alert; you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception.

name: Twilight Halfling
description: Your vision adapted to dim light long ago; you gain low-light vision.

name: Wildwood Halfling
description: You grew up in a forest; you ignore difficult terrain from non-magical plants and undergrowth.

name: Half-Elf
description: One of your parents was an elf; you have pointed ears, gain low-light vision, and can select elf ancestry feats.

name: Half-Orc
description: One of your parents was an orc; you have a tusked face, gain darkvision, and can select orc ancestry feats.

name: Skilled Heritage
description: Your human adaptability manifests as extra training; you become trained in one additional skill at 1st level.

name: Versatile Heritage
description: You gain an additional 1st-level general feat, reflecting the breadth of human potential.

name: Cactus Leshy
description: Spines grow from your body; adjacent creatures that hit you with unarmed strikes take 1 piercing damage.

name: Fungus Leshy
description: You are made of fungal matter; you gain darkvision and resistance to poison damage.

name: Gourd Leshy
description: Your hollow gourd body has a small cavity; you can store one light item inside without it taking up a hand.

name: Leaf Leshy
description: Broad leaves make up much of your body; you always land safely from falls and can glide to the ground.

name: Root Leshy
description: Stout roots and dense wood make you harder to move; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus against Shove and Trip.

name: Seaweed Leshy
description: You are made of aquatic plant matter; you gain a swim Speed of 20 feet and can breathe water.

name: Vine Leshy
description: Flexible vines make up your body; your reach with unarmed attacks is 10 feet instead of 5 feet.

name: Badlands Orc
description: At home in arid badlands, you have resistance to fire damage and need only half as much water as normal.

name: Battle-Ready Orc
description: Prepared for combat from an early age, you are trained with all martial weapons.

name: Deep Orc
description: You hail from subterranean orc communities; you gain darkvision and light blindness.

name: Grave Orc
description: You have an affinity with death magic; void healing and vitality damage affect you as if you were undead.

name: Hold-Scarred Orc
description: Ritual scarification toughens your hide; you gain resistance to piercing and slashing damage equal to half your level.

name: Rainfall Orc
description: From a rainy coastal culture, you gain a swim Speed of 15 feet and resistance to cold damage.

name: Venomous Orc
description: Poisonous secretions make your bite venomous; your bite Strike has the poison trait and deals 1 poison damage on a hit.

name: Clawed Catfolk
description: Your claws are especially large and sharp; your claw Strike deals 1d6 slashing damage.

name: Hunting Catfolk
description: You are a born hunter; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics and Acrobatics to move through an enemy's space.

name: Jungle Catfolk
description: Adapted to humid jungles, you ignore difficult terrain from non-magical undergrowth and gain a climb Speed of 10 feet.

name: Nine Lives Catfolk
description: Your legendary luck manifests as an ability to avoid death; once per day you can survive an attack that would kill you.

name: Sharp-Eared Catfolk
description: Your ears are especially keen; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks involving hearing.

name: Bright Fetchling
description: You reflect the inverse light of the Shadow Plane; you emit dim light in a 10-foot radius, which you can suppress.

name: Deep Shadow Fetchling
description: Especially connected to shadow, you can Dim the Light as a free action, reducing light level by one step in a 10-foot aura.

name: Liminal Fetchling
description: You straddle the boundary between planes; once per hour you can Step into a shadow to teleport up to 10 feet.

name: Resolute Fetchling
description: Exposure to the Shadow Plane has steeled your mind; you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against mental effects.

name: Wisp Fetchling
description: Wisps of shadow trail behind you; once per day you can become invisible for 1 round as a free action.

name: Cavern Kobold
description: You dwell in underground tunnels; you gain darkvision and can squeeze through spaces as if you were Tiny sized.

name: Dragonscaled Kobold
description: Your scales are thicker than most kobolds'; you gain a +1 item bonus to AC when unarmored.

name: Strongjaw Kobold
description: Your bite is especially powerful for a kobold; your fangs Strike deals 1d6 piercing damage.

name: Venomtail Kobold
description: A venomous spur on your tail lets you poison enemies; your tail spur Strike deals poison damage on a hit.

name: Wingless Kobold
description: Despite lacking wings, you are exceptionally quick; you gain a +5-foot circumstance bonus to your Speed.

name: Deep Rat
description: From underground warrens, you gain darkvision and a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against poison.

name: Desert Rat
description: Adapted to arid deserts, you have fire resistance and need only half as much water as other characters.

name: Shadow Rat
description: Connected to shadow creatures, you can see perfectly in magical darkness as well as mundane darkness (greater darkvision).

name: Sewer Rat
description: Living in filth has made you resilient; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against disease and poison.

name: Tunnel Rat
description: Adept at moving through narrow passages, you can move at full Speed while squeezing and fit through Tiny-sized spaces.

name: Dogtooth Tengu
description: Your beak has a distinctive serrated hook; your beak Strike deals 1d6 piercing damage instead of 1d4.

name: Jinxed Tengu
description: You attract misfortune, but can redirect it; once per day when you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it.

name: Jinx-Eater Tengu
description: You can absorb curses afflicting allies; once per day you can transfer a curse from an adjacent ally to yourself.

name: Long-Nosed Tengu
description: Your elongated beak can detect lies; you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception to Sense Motive.

name: Mountainkeeper Tengu
description: You hail from high mountain communities; you are acclimated to high altitudes and gain cold resistance.

name: Wavediver Tengu
description: Adapted to aquatic environments, you gain a swim Speed of 20 feet and can breathe both air and water.
